research and design is a strategic and necessary component for most it development and failure to recognize and develop mature ux capabilities will result in inferior products and solutions because cons: trashiest place i have ever worked i waited 11 months to write this review and i'm still pissed as hell 1 my performance reviews were great up for promotion and tcs took away decision rights from practice leaders in north america and fired me with a zero day notice and gave ocm practice leadership no say in the decisions nor did they even notify practice leadership or my manager that i was being let go company has also been in ongoing court case of discrimination in the u s with prioritization of hb1 visas which is unfortunate to say b c there is plenty of opportunity for everyone but leadership out of india has driven some incredibly poor workforce decisions 2 when confronted about the firing process the us head of hr stated performance reviews were not used in the layoff process when asked then what criteria was used hr revealed deep biases 3 i should have read the pink flags where they were when i interviewed with tsc when i asked what tcs was doing from a deib perspective my interviewer said oh yeah tcs has written a paper about it - which emphasized a lack of commitment from tcs to deib altogether 4 tcs is a massive black hole no leader takes responsibility for anything it is the greatest environment of cowardice i have ever seen in my career and i've worked with great places including deloitte and multiple fortune 100s in industry 5 the worst it support you could imagine - ironically they blame it on culture for india but in reality many companies have very successful support teams in india - so it's a matter of failure in leadership being able to set up shared services in a way that supports billable talent 6 clients can tell easily where tcs lacks in skills and capabilities compared to other firms and have to lean in more heavily 7 in 3 years i never saw any strong pmo leaders on programs promises without meeting them client delays regularly just a mess also no integration of workstreams in dependency planning

title: Stable income and I was lucky to work remotely
pros: stable income and i was lucky to work remotely
cons: i've had a challenging experience working at tcs and there are several key areas that have contributed to my dissatisfaction with the company communication one of the biggest issues i've encountered is the poor communication within the organization it was often difficult to find the right person to reach out to for help or guidance which made navigating the company frustrating my manager rarely communicated with me leaving me feeling unsupported and disconnected from the team project assignment despite being hired as a software engineer i was placed on a project that felt more like it support work the tasks i was assigned were not aligned with the role i was hired for and the project lacked value in terms of contributing to my growth as a developer compensation the pay at tcs is low especially considering the expectations and demands of the role this made it difficult to feel motivated or valued as an employee appraisal system the appraisal system at tcs is another area that i found problematic the process involves setting goals in a portal but the evaluation of your performance is done by someone who you may have never worked with during the project this random evaluation determines your grade and in turn your bonus which feels unfair and disconnected from the actual work you ve done overall my experience at tcs has been disappointing due to these factors i believe there are significant improvements that could be made in communication project alignment compensation and performance evaluation to create a more supportive and rewarding environment for employees
